The '''Noehons''' were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] arboreal [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] indigenous to [[Noe'ha'on/Legends|Noe'ha'on]]. Noehons were well-known throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] and slave-traders. Their culture was often considered brutal and violent, though Noehons raised away from Noe'ha'on's rigid, hierarchical society showed a talent for organization which served them well in technical and bureaucratic fields.

==Biology and appearance==
Noehons had large [[Color/Legends|red]] compound [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], with each facet responsible for a different aspect of vision; such as motion or the fine details of nearby objects. They had large mandibles, and a soft, bristly carapace, which was mottled green in color. They walked on two [[Limb/Legends|legs]], and had four arms with which they could use tools or brachiate through the jungles of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Since they were used to hanging upside-down in [[Tree/Legends|trees]], they were resistant to the disorientation and [[motion sickness]], which afflicted some species in zero-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] conditions.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

Every Noehon had a pair of scent glands, one of each side of their mouth, which produced a strong, musky scent. Each individual Noehon had a unique scent, which they used to mark possessions, territory, and their subordinates. The scent was difficult to remove, even with repeated washing. Noehons were typically possessive, frequently marking items in their possession and rarely discarding items which they thought might be useful later. They also tended to organize their possessions fastidiously, ensuring everything was in its proper place.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

==Society==
[[File:Noehons.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Noehon]]
Noehon society was based on tightly-knit communities called ''welds'', containing anywhere from a few hundred to ten thousand members. Each weld was ruled by a single dominant "alpha" [[Gender/Legends|male]]. The alpha male's "harem" of females were treated as commodities, with excess adolescent females traded to other welds for technology and other valuables. Some welds would steal females from smaller, poorly defended welds. Noehon females also organized themselves hierarchically, with the oldest females dominating the youngest or most recently captured females.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

Adolescent males in the weld were driven off by the dominant male as soon as they came of age. Elderly male leaders would usually be killed by a younger male, who then took control of the weld. Despite the rigidity of gender roles within the weld, Noehons did not treat female and male offworlders differently. Even among offworlders, however, they understood society in a hierarchical way: bullying subordinates while fearing and trying to appease superiors. Noehons had a natural talent for managing others through intimidation.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

Noehon males who had never established or taken control of a weld were often less domineering, and lived a solitary existence on Noe'ha'on. Others went offworld to establish their dominance over slaves of other species.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" /> Females such as [[Tir'uh]], one of the leaders of the [[Mytaranor Slaving Council]], also went offworld to take up the slave trade.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> Female Noehon slavers were sometimes crueler masters than males, even though they usually began their own lives as chattel.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

==History==
[[File:Noehon.png|thumb|right|200px|A Noehon is attacked by [[Puln]], A [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] operative.]]

The Noehons never developed spaceflight or [[Galactic community|galactic]]-level technology on their own, though they did develop their own crude industrial technology powered by wood and fossil fuels. Visiting [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] traded advanced technology to the welds, including [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and [[Vibro-weapon/Legends|vibroweapons]], in exchange for Noehon slaves, medicinal products, and natural resources. With a glut of advanced weapons replacing Noehon [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrowers]], tribal rivalries turned into violent conflicts which only ended when one weld was utterly destroyed.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

Though the vast majority of Noehons remained on their homeworld, slaves and descendants of slaves were found throughout the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Noehons were able to adapt to life on many worlds, as well as zero-gravity conditions. Noehon raised outside a weld were calmer than their counterparts from their homeworld, and could integrate more successfully with the galactic community.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the brutality of Noehon culture fit in well with the needs of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] war machine. Other Noehons, especially those raised outside of welds, used their fastidious organizational talents to help the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 12" /> One such Noehon was [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Hark'r]], supply master of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic's]] [[Home|''Home'' base]].<ref name="Classic Campaigns">''[[Classic Campaigns]]''</ref>
